name: Siam (1855–1916)

colors.symbolism: the white elephant, the auspicious royal beast of Siam

description: A red field bearing a plain white elephant (facing the hoist) at the centre, without the chakra.
history: Created in 1855 by Rama IV, who placed a white elephant alone on the red field so that Siam's flag would be distinct enough for international relations. It served as the national flag until 1916.
